headline: Dell, Super Micro, or HPE: Which AI Server Stock Crushed It in April?
summary: The April scoreboard is in for the three biggest pure plays on artificial intelligence (AI) server hardware, and the spread between first and last is wider than the headline gains suggest. Shares of Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL) closed the month up 28%, pulling away from Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) at 21% and Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) ... Dell, Super Micro, or HPE: Which AI Server Stock Crushed It in April?

headline: 3 Growth Stocks With High Insider Ownership Achieving 19% Revenue Growth
summary: Over the last 7 days, the United States market has remained flat, yet it is up 29% over the past year with earnings forecast to grow by 16% annually. In this environment, identifying growth stocks with high insider ownership can be appealing as these companies often benefit from strong alignment between management and shareholders, potentially driving sustainable revenue growth.

headline: Should You Hold or Fold Super Micro Computer Stock Before Q3 Earnings?
summary: SMCI eyes explosive Q3 growth on AI server demand, but margin pressure and cash cycle issues may temper investor optimism.

headline: Is It Time To Reconsider Super Micro Computer (SMCI) After Recent Share Price Swings?
summary: Investors may be wondering if Super Micro Computer's share price still reflects its underlying worth, or if the recent swings have left it mispriced. Over the past week the stock return is 2.4%, over the last month it is 20.3%, while year to date it shows an 11.5% decline and the one year return is a 16.3% decline, set against a very large 3 year gain and a roughly 7x 5 year gain. These moves sit against ongoing interest in data center hardware and AI related infrastructure, where investors...
